input.name: Fix native runner start
input.overview: `ghr up` fails on Linux native runners because bash parses `a && b & c` as `(a && b) & c`, which backgrounds the entire chain including `cd`. That breaks the script and yields `bash: line 1: : No such file or directory` with exit 1. Fix by grouping only the background start in a subshell and emitting the PID message from that subshell.
input.plan:
# Fix native Linux `ghr up` bash operator precedence bug

## Root cause

The native start one-liner is built in [`nativeUnixStartShell`](internal/runner/native.go) as:

```go
`... && nohup ./run.sh > runner.log 2>&1 & echo $! > "$pid_file" && echo "started PID $!"`
```

In bash, **`&` has the same list-separator precedence as `;`**, not lower than `&&`. So the parser treats this as:

```text
( cd ... && ... && nohup ./run.sh > runner.log 2>&1 ) & echo $! > ...
```

The long `cd … && … && nohup …` runs in a **background subshell**, while `echo $! > ".runner_pid"` runs in the **parent** (SSH login shell), which **never changed directory**. That leads to broken behavior; in a minimal reproduction the same stderr appears:

`bash: line 1: : No such file or directory` and exit status **1** (matches your log).

## Fix

Change [`nativeUnixStartShell`](internal/runner/native.go) so that:

1. **`cd` and the PID-file “already running” check stay in the foreground** (unchanged logic).
2. **Only** the `nohup ./run.sh` + PID write run inside a **subshell** with explicit sequencing, e.g.:

```sh
( nohup ./run.sh > runner.log 2>&1 & pid=$!; echo $pid > "$pid_file"; echo "started PID $pid" )
```

- The subshell **inherits the cwd** after `cd`, so paths stay correct.
- `pid=$!` and the `echo`s run in the same subshell after the `&`, so `$!` is the runner process.
- The final user-visible line is printed from inside the subshell (stdout is still what `h.Run` captures).

## Tests

Update [`Test_nativeUnixStartShell_usesQuotedDirAndPidNormalization`](internal/runner/native_test.go) (and any related assertions) to expect the new grouping (`(` before `nohup`, `pid=$!`, etc.) instead of the fragile `& echo $! > ... && echo "started PID $!"` tail.

## Verification

- `go test ./internal/runner/...` (and optionally full `go test ./...`).
- Manual: `ghr up` against a native Linux host should start the runner without the empty-command error.

todos.content: Rewrite nativeUnixStartShell in internal/runner/native.go with subshell (nohup & pid=$!; write pid; echo message)
Adjust internal/runner/native_test.go assertions for new script shape

content.text:
Here’s what was implemented:

### Change in `nativeUnixStartShell` (`internal/runner/native.go`)

Bash was parsing `… && nohup ./run.sh … & echo $! …` as **`(cd … && nohup …) & echo $!`**, so `cd` ran in a background subshell and the parent never entered the runner directory, which led to `bash: line 1: : No such file or directory` and exit 1.

The start step is now wrapped in a **subshell** so only `nohup` is backgrounded inside a shell that already has the right cwd:

```512:517:internal/runner/native.go
		`cd %s || exit 1 && pid_file=".runner_pid" && `+
			`if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then pid=$(tr -d '\r' < "$pid_file" | head -n 1); `+
			`if [ -n "$pid" ] && kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then echo "already running"; exit 0; fi; fi && `+
			`( nohup ./run.sh > runner.log 2>&1 & pid=$!; echo $pid > "$pid_file"; echo "started PID $pid" )`,
		quotedDir,
```

### Tests (`internal/runner/native_test.go`)

- Assert the `( nohup ./run.sh`, `pid=$!`, and `echo "started PID $pid"` pattern.
- Reject the old unsafe `& echo $! >` form.

`go test ./internal/runner/...` completed successfully (exit 0). You can rerun `ghr up` on the native Linux host to confirm end-to-end.
